findings.evidence: `rg` shows the public definition at `Complex.lean:52` and only `transposeEquiv_single`/`transposeEquiv_apply` at `Complex.lean:62` and `Complex.lean:71`; the parallel rectangle API includes inverse application `transposeEquiv_symm_apply` at `Rectangle.lean:713`.
findings.fix: Add inverse-side characteristic lemmas, e.g. `@[simp] theorem transposeEquiv_symm_single ... : (transposeEquiv R n).symm (Finsupp.single x a) = Finsupp.single x.transpose a` and `@[simp] theorem transposeEquiv_symm_apply ... : (transposeEquiv R n).symm f y = f y.transpose`, or an equivalent `@[simp]` theorem identifying the inverse with `transposeEquiv`.
findings.issue: `GridChain.transposeEquiv` is exposed as a linear automorphism, but its API only has forward lemmas `transposeEquiv_single` and `transposeEquiv_apply`; there are no `@[simp]` lemmas for `(transposeEquiv R n).symm` even though the inverse is the same transpose relabeling.

summary: The rectangle-level transpose API now has the expected equivalence and cancellation lemmas, but the newly exposed chain relabeling automorphism is only characterized in the forward direction. Users needing the inverse map for naturality-style arguments must unfold `GridChain.transposeEquiv`, which leaves the public API incomplete.
